Read the codebase and explain the message-routing architecture in relay from message ingestion through queueing or routing decisions to final delivery. Base your answer only on code you actually read.

tests.stdout:
> agent-relay@3.1.19 pretest
> npm run build


> agent-relay@3.1.19 build
> npm run clean && npm run build:rust && turbo run build --filter='./packages/*' && tsc


> agent-relay@3.1.19 clean
> rm -rf dist && find packages -maxdepth 2 -name dist -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true


> agent-relay@3.1.19 build:rust
> if command -v ~/.cargo/bin/cargo >/dev/null 2>&1; then ~/.cargo/bin/cargo build --release --bin agent-relay-broker && mkdir -p packages/sdk/bin && cp target/release/agent-relay-broker packages/sdk/bin/agent-relay-broker.new && mv -f packages/sdk/bin/agent-relay-broker.new packages/sdk/bin/agent-relay-broker && echo '✓ broker binary (agent-relay-broker) built and copied to packages/sdk/bin/'; else echo '⚠ Rust not installed, using prebuilt binaries from bin/'; fi

strengths: Extremely detailed with specific line numbers and code snippets from actual file reads
Covers the full pipeline: ingestion → mapping → routing → queueing → delivery → retry

judge.summary: The response provides a thorough, well-structured trace of the relay message-routing architecture from WebSocket ingestion through mapping, routing decisions, priority queueing, and delivery with retry. It references specific files (relaycast_ws.rs, routing.rs, queue.rs, message_bridge.rs, main.rs) with line numbers and plausible code snippets grounded in 13 file reads. However, it omits src/protocol.rs entirely—one of four explicitly required modules—missing the protocol interpretation layer that the ground truth highlights. The remaining coverage is strong: workspace isolation, self-echo detection, DM participant resolution, priority-based overflow, and retry semantics are all addressed with concrete code evidence.
weaknesses: Missing src/protocol.rs entirely—a required module for protocol interpretation and transport concerns
Does not address where protocol objects are interpreted, an explicit ground-truth expectation
Some code snippets may be paraphrased or reconstructed rather than exact copies, making verification harder

notes: Supports the special relay baseline condition.

acceptance_criteria: Explains ingress, routing, buffering/queueing, and delivery in order.
Uses real relay module names and boundaries.
Mentions protocol interpretation and transport concerns where relevant.
Avoids inventing services or queues not present in the codebase.
task.ground_truth:
Expected outcome:
- Must trace the major routing stages across the broker/runtime, identify where protocol objects are interpreted, and explain where delivery paths diverge. Expected grounding includes src/routing.rs, src/queue.rs, src/protocol.rs, and relaycast WebSocket delivery surfaces.

Must mention / touch:
- src/routing.rs
- src/queue.rs
- src/protocol.rs
- src/relaycast_ws.rs

Common failure modes:
- Missing one of the major layers or participants
- Describing systems or modules that are not evidenced in the repo
